user_rights: You have the right to return defective goods and claim a full refund within 30 days.
steps: Contact seller support with order ID and proof
Escalate to platform grievance officer within 48 hours
File complaint on National Consumer Helpline (1800-11-4000)
Approach District Consumer Commission if unresolved in 30 days

user_rights: Right to be protected against marketing of goods which are hazardous to life and property.
steps: Document the counterfeit item with photos and videos
Report to the e-commerce platform immediately
Demand an immediate refund and pickup of the fake item
If refused, file a complaint on the National Consumer Helpline
authorities: Local Police (if fraud is high value)

user_rights: Right to receive goods within the promised timeframe or seek compensation for delay.
steps: Check tracking status and contact courier company
Raise a dispute with the e-commerce platform
If delayed beyond 15 days of promised date, demand cancellation and full refund
Lodge a complaint on NCH if refund is denied

user_rights: Right to receive the exact product as described on the platform.
steps: Take unboxing video if possible
Initiate a return request immediately
Upload photos of the wrong item received
Contact customer care if the return is rejected

user_rights: No person shall sell any pre-packaged commodity at a price exceeding the maximum retail price.
steps: Keep the bill showing the price above MRP
Complain to the store manager
Report to the Legal Metrology Department of your state
File a complaint on the National Consumer Helpline
documents_required: Photo of MRP on the product

user_rights: Zero liability if reported within 3 working days of the unauthorized transaction.
steps: Block the card/account immediately through the bank app or helpline
Report to the bank within 3 days to ensure zero liability
File a complaint on cybercrime.gov.in
Obtain an acknowledgement from the bank and police
physical_visit_instructions: Visit your home branch to submit a formal written dispute form and a copy of the FIR/Cyber complaint.

user_rights: Right to secure payment systems and redressal for fraudulent transactions.
steps: Immediately report the transaction in the UPI app (GPay/PhonePe)
Call the national cybercrime helpline 1930
Report to your bank and the beneficiary bank
File a formal complaint on the RBI CMS portal

user_rights: Bank must reverse the transaction within T+5 days or pay ₹100 per day penalty.
steps: Wait for 24 hours for auto-reversal
If not reversed, file a complaint with your bank
Note the ATM ID and transaction time
Escalate to the Banking Ombudsman if not resolved in 30 days

user_rights: Right to transparency in banking charges and fair treatment.
steps: Identify the specific charge in your statement
Request a clarification from the bank manager
If charges are against RBI guidelines, demand a reversal
File a complaint with the Banking Ombudsman

applicable_law: RBI Fair Practices Code for Lenders
user_rights: Recovery agents cannot call before 8 AM or after 7 PM, nor can they use physical or mental harassment.
steps: Record the calls and keep evidence of harassment
File a formal complaint with the bank's Nodal Officer
Report to the local police if there are threats
Complain to the RBI via the CMS portal
physical_visit_instructions: Visit the local police station to file a complaint if you face physical threats or intimidation.

user_rights: Right to full refund or alternative flight plus compensation for cancellations within certain timeframes.
steps: Ask the airline for the reason for cancellation in writing
Check if you are entitled to free meals or hotel stay
Request a full refund if the alternative flight is not acceptable
File a complaint on AirSewa portal if the airline refuses

user_rights: Right to compensation for lost or damaged baggage based on weight and rules.
steps: File a Property Irregularity Report (PIR) before leaving the airport
Take photos of the damaged luggage immediately
Submit a formal claim for compensation within 7 days
Escalate to AirSewa if the claim is ignored
physical_visit_instructions: You must file the PIR report at the airline's baggage counter before exiting the arrivals hall.

user_rights: Right to refund if the train is delayed by more than 3 hours and the passenger does not travel.
steps: Check the live status of the train
File a Ticket Deposit Receipt (TDR) online through IRCTC
Mention 'Train Delayed More Than Three Hours' as the reason
Use RailMadad app for real-time complaints during travel

user_rights: Right to receive services as promised and refund for deficiency in service.
steps: Take photos/videos of poor conditions
Contact the booking platform (MakeMyTrip/OYO etc.)
Ask for a room upgrade or relocation
If unresolved, file a complaint on the National Consumer Helpline

user_rights: Right to be protected against unfair trade practices by travel agents.
steps: Verify the agent's credentials and registration
Request a detailed itinerary and payment breakdown
File a complaint with the Ministry of Tourism if the agent is registered
Approach the Consumer Commission for refund of the package cost

user_rights: Service charge is voluntary. Restaurants cannot add it to the bill by default.
steps: Point out the CCPA guidelines to the manager
Request the removal of the service charge from the bill
If they refuse, pay the bill and note your protest on it
File a complaint on the National Consumer Helpline (1800-11-4000)
documents_required: Photo of menu showing service charge (if any)

user_rights: Right to safe and wholesome food; compensation for injury caused by unsafe food.
steps: Preserve the food sample and take photos
Keep the medical reports and bills if you fell ill
File a complaint on the FSSAI FoSCoS portal
Report to the local health officer/municipal corporation
physical_visit_instructions: Visit a government hospital to get a medical certificate if you suffer from food poisoning to strengthen your case.

user_rights: Right to a valid GST invoice; restaurants cannot charge GST if not registered.
steps: Check if the GSTIN is printed on the bill
Verify the GSTIN on the GST portal
If incorrect, report it to the GST department
File a complaint on the CBIC Mitra portal

user_rights: Right to receive the food as ordered or get a refund for incorrect/damaged items.
steps: Take a photo of the spilled/incorrect food immediately
Raise a dispute in the Zomato/Swiggy app chat
Escalate to their customer support team
If unresolved, file a complaint on NCH

applicable_law: Food Safety and Standards (Packaging and Labelling) Regulations
user_rights: Right to be informed about the expiry date and not be sold expired products.
steps: Check the 'Best Before' or 'Expiry' date on the package
Do not consume the product
Report the store to FSSAI
Seek a refund from the retailer
documents_required: Photo of expiry date on package

user_rights: Right to transparent billing and to see the rate list of the hospital.
steps: Request an itemized bill from the hospital
Compare the charges with the hospital's published rate list
Raise the issue with the hospital's grievance cell
File a complaint with the State Medical Council

user_rights: Right to receive professional care; right to compensation for medical negligence.
steps: Obtain all medical records and discharge summaries
Get an independent opinion from another doctor
File a formal complaint with the State Medical Council
Approach the Consumer Commission for compensation
physical_visit_instructions: You may need to appear before the Medical Council committee to explain your case and provide evidence.

applicable_law: Article 21 of the Constitution (Right to Life)
user_rights: Hospitals must provide emergency treatment regardless of the patient's ability to pay immediately.
steps: Document the refusal (video or witness statements)
Seek treatment at the nearest government hospital immediately
Report the private hospital to the State Health Department
File a writ petition in the High Court if it's a grave violation

user_rights: Hospitals cannot detain a dead body or a patient due to unpaid bills.
steps: Inform the hospital that detaining the body is illegal
Call the local police for immediate assistance
File a complaint with the District Magistrate
Settle the dispute later through the Consumer Commission
physical_visit_instructions: Immediate police intervention at the hospital is usually necessary to resolve this situation.

user_rights: Right to fair claim settlement and clear reasons for rejection.
steps: Review the rejection letter from the TPA/Insurance company
Gather medical evidence to counter the rejection reason
File an appeal with the insurance company's grievance officer
Approach the Insurance Ombudsman if unresolved

user_rights: Right to contest a challan in court if you believe it was wrongly issued.
steps: Do not pay the challan immediately if you wish to contest it
Check the evidence (photo/video) attached to the challan online
Wait for the challan to be sent to the Virtual Court
Submit your contest/explanation on the Virtual Court portal

user_rights: Right to claim compensation from the Motor Accident Claims Tribunal (MACT).
steps: Note the registration number of the other vehicle
File an FIR at the nearest police station
Inform your insurance company within 24 hours
File a claim petition in the MACT with the help of a lawyer
physical_visit_instructions: You must visit the police station to file the FIR and the MACT court for the compensation proceedings.

user_rights: Towing must be done as per procedure; authorities are liable for damage caused during towing.
steps: Take photos of the damage at the towing yard
Inform the yard officer and note it in their records
File a formal complaint with the Traffic Police Commissioner
Seek compensation for repairs through the Consumer Commission
physical_visit_instructions: Visit the traffic police headquarters to file a formal complaint against the towing contractor.

user_rights: Right to receive RTO services within the prescribed time under the Citizen's Charter.
steps: Check the application status on the Parivahan portal
Raise a grievance on the Parivahan portal
Visit the RTO and meet the Public Relations Officer (PRO)
File a complaint on the state's Jan Grievance portal
physical_visit_instructions: A visit to the RTO might be necessary to meet the officer in charge of your application.

user_rights: Right to be informed about the true condition of the vehicle; protection against fraud.
steps: Get a mechanical inspection report
Check the vehicle history on the Parivahan portal
Send a legal notice to the seller for misrepresentation
File a civil suit for recovery of money

user_rights: Right to accurate billing and 24/7 customer support for grievance redressal.
steps: Call the customer care (198) and get a complaint number
If not resolved in 3 days, escalate to the Appellate Authority of the operator
Wait for 10 days for their decision
File a complaint on the TRAI portal if still unresolved

applicable_law: TRAI Broadband Quality of Service Regulations
user_rights: Right to receive at least 80% of the promised broadband speed.
steps: Run speed tests and take screenshots
Log a complaint with the ISP and get a ticket number
Demand a pro-rata refund for the downtime period
Escalate to the ISP's nodal officer

user_rights: Operator is responsible for ensuring secure SIM verification processes.
steps: Immediately contact the operator to block the SIM
Report to the bank if mobile banking is linked
File a complaint on the Cyber Crime Portal
Hold the telecom operator liable for negligence
physical_visit_instructions: Visit the operator's store with your ID proof to regain control of your mobile number.

applicable_law: TRAI Telecom Commercial Communications Customer Preference Regulations
user_rights: Right to not receive unsolicited commercial communications if registered on DND.
steps: Register for DND by sending 'FULL DND' to 1909
Report spam SMS by forwarding to 1909 in the format: 'Spam, Number, Date'
Use the TRAI DND 3.0 app for easy reporting
Operator must take action against the telemarketer within 7 days

user_rights: Right to data protection and to be informed about data breaches.
steps: Ask the company for details of the data breach
Report to the Data Protection Board (once fully operational)
Change all passwords and enable 2FA
Seek compensation if the breach causes financial loss

user_rights: Right to refund of fees if the coaching center fails to provide promised services or if a student leaves mid-course (subject to terms).
steps: Review the enrollment agreement and refund policy
Send a formal written request for a refund to the center
If refused, send a legal notice citing deficiency in service
File a complaint with the Consumer Commission

user_rights: Right to receive degrees and marksheets within a reasonable time after completion of the course.
steps: Apply formally for the document and keep the receipt
File an RTI application to know the status and reason for delay
Meet the Controller of Examinations at the University
File a complaint with the UGC or the Governor (Chancellor of the University)
physical_visit_instructions: You may need to visit the university administrative block to follow up on your application.

user_rights: Protection against fraudulent educational institutions.
steps: Check the UGC list of fake universities online
Demand a full refund of all fees paid
File an FIR for cheating and fraud
Approach the Consumer Commission for compensation
physical_visit_instructions: Visit the local police station to file a criminal complaint against the institution owners.

user_rights: Schools are prohibited from charging capitation fees or demanding donations for admission.
steps: Record any demand for donation if possible
File a complaint with the District Education Officer (DEO)
Report to the State Commission for Protection of Child Rights (SCPCR)
Approach the High Court if the school takes retaliatory action

user_rights: Right to safe accommodation and refund of security deposits as per agreement.
steps: Document poor conditions with photos
Send a written notice for refund of deposit
Check if the hostel is registered with local authorities
File a complaint on NCH if the deposit is withheld without reason

applicable_law: Model Tenancy Act / State Rent Control Act
user_rights: Landlord must return the security deposit within one month of vacating, after valid deductions.
steps: Give valid notice as per the rent agreement
Take photos of the vacant house to prove no damage
Send a formal demand letter for the deposit
Approach the Rent Authority or Civil Court for recovery

applicable_law: Section 106 of the Transfer of Property Act 1882
user_rights: Tenant cannot be evicted without due process of law and proper notice.
steps: Call the local police if the landlord tries to forcibly evict you
File for an injunction in the Civil Court to stop the eviction
Show your valid rent agreement and latest rent receipts
Report the matter to the Rent Controller
physical_visit_instructions: You must visit the civil court to file a suit for injunction and the police station for protection.

user_rights: Right to timely possession or refund with interest for delays by the builder.
steps: Check the project registration on the State RERA website
File a formal complaint on the RERA portal
Attend the hearings conducted by the RERA authority
Execute the RERA order through the District Collector if the builder doesn't pay
physical_visit_instructions: You may need to attend hearings at the RERA office in your state capital.

applicable_law: Societies Registration Act / Apartment Ownership Act
user_rights: Right to transparent accounts and fair maintenance charges as decided by the General Body.
steps: Request the audit reports and breakdown of maintenance charges
Raise the issue in the Annual General Body Meeting (AGM)
File a complaint with the Registrar of Societies
Approach the Consumer Commission for deficiency in service

user_rights: Landlord is responsible for structural repairs and cannot cut essential services.
steps: Send a written notice to the landlord for repairs
If they fail, apply to the Rent Controller for permission to repair and deduct from rent
If electricity/water is cut, file an urgent application with the Rent Authority
Police can also intervene in case of cut-off of essential services
physical_visit_instructions: Visit the Rent Authority office to file an application for restoration of essential services.

user_rights: Right to receive passport services within the time specified in the Citizen's Charter.
steps: Check the status on the Passport Seva portal
Visit the Passport Seva Kendra (PSK) during inquiry hours
Raise a grievance on the CPGRAMS portal
Contact the Regional Passport Officer (RPO) via Twitter or email
physical_visit_instructions: Visit the RPO office for a personal interview if your passport is stuck for a long time.

user_rights: Right to timely disbursement of pension and provident fund benefits.
steps: Check the status on the EPFO/Pension portal
Log a complaint on the EPFiGMS portal
File an RTI to know the reason for delay and the names of officers responsible
Escalate to the Central Grievance Portal (CPGRAMS)

user_rights: Right to services without paying bribes; protection for whistleblowers.
steps: Do not pay the bribe
Record the conversation or demand if possible
Contact the Anti-Corruption Bureau (ACB) of your state
Report to the Central Vigilance Commission (CVC) online
physical_visit_instructions: You must visit the ACB office to file a formal complaint and participate in a trap if they suggest it.

user_rights: Right to get information within 30 days of application.
steps: Wait for 30 days from the date of application
If no response, file a First Appeal with the First Appellate Authority (FAA)
If FAA also doesn't respond in 45 days, file a Second Appeal with the Information Commission
Demand a penalty of ₹250 per day for the delay

user_rights: Right to basic civic amenities as a taxpayer.
steps: Register a complaint on the local municipality's app (e.g., Swachhata app)
Take photos of the issue and post on social media tagging the authority
Meet the Ward Councilor or the Zonal Commissioner
File a complaint on the state's unified grievance portal
